design: Cal-Manip-Cal sandwich, 30 paired prompts x 5 conditions (A=this, B=a, C=your, D=the, E=their), cold cache
calibration_paragraph: Transformer models process input text through a sequence of layers. Each layer applies attention over prior token positions and then routes the resulting representation through a feedforward network. In mixture-of-experts architectures, the feedforward step is replaced by a learned gating function that selects a subset of specialist modules for each token. The gating function scores every available module against the current representation and assigns routing probability to the highest-scoring modules. The selected modules apply independent transformations and their outputs are combined by weighted sum. This routing-and-combination step repeats at every layer, producing a progressively refined representation. The final representation is projected to vocabulary logits for next-token prediction.

categories.name: social
categories.description: Cat social behavior and group dynamics

categories.name: instinct
categories.description: Cat innate and instinctive behavior patterns

pairs.A: This cat processes sensory information from its environment, integrating patterns of scent and sound to distinguish familiar from unfamiliar stimuli. The behavioral responses of this cat emerge through learned associations refined over repeated interactions with its surroundings. When this cat encounters a novel scent marker, the olfactory neurons of this cat relay information that shapes the approach or avoidance response.

pairs.A: This cat tracks moving objects using peripheral vision refined by generations of selective breeding for pursuit tasks. The visual cortex of this cat processes motion at higher temporal resolution than color, allowing this cat to detect the trajectory of a thrown object before a human observer registers the throw. The retinal response of this cat favors contrast gradients over fine spatial detail.

pairs.A: This cat detects frequencies beyond the range of human hearing, filtering ambient noise to isolate relevant acoustic patterns. The directional hearing of this cat allows localization of a sound source within a few degrees of arc. When this cat hears a familiar voice, the auditory pathway of this cat activates recognition circuits before the vocalization finishes.

pairs.A: This cat responds to pressure applied along its spine with a reflexive lateral movement calibrated by prior handling experience. pairs.A: This cat maintains balance on uneven surfaces by integrating vestibular input with proprioceptive feedback from its limbs. The postural adjustments of this cat happen continuously and without apparent effort during locomotion. When this cat navigates a narrow ledge, the motor cortex of this cat coordinates limb placement using sensory predictions updated at each step.

pairs.A: This cat combines visual, auditory, and olfactory information to build a coherent representation of its immediate surroundings. The brain of this cat resolves conflicts between sensory channels by weighting the most reliable modality for each context. pairs.A: This cat establishes social rank through ritualized displays rather than sustained physical aggression toward other group members. pairs.A: This cat initiates play using a bow posture that signals nonaggressive intent to a potential interaction partner. The play sequences of this cat include role reversals where the dominant individual voluntarily yields to sustain the interaction. When this cat detects that a partner has disengaged, the solicitation behavior of this cat escalates until reciprocal engagement resumes.

pairs.A: This cat forms attachment bonds with primary caregivers through repeated positive interactions over an early sensitive developmental period. pairs.A: This cat marks territorial boundaries using scent deposits placed at regular intervals along familiar patrol routes. pairs.A: This cat coordinates with human handlers during search tasks by alternating between independent exploration and handler directed movement. pairs.A: This cat de-escalates confrontations through appeasement signals including averted gaze and deliberately lowered body posture. The calming signals of this cat function to reduce emotional arousal in both the signaler and the recipient simultaneously. When this cat encounters a tense situation between two other animals, the intervention behavior of this cat often involves physically interposing between the antagonists.

pairs.A: This cat gradually ceases a previously reinforced behavior when the expected reinforcement is consistently withheld over multiple sessions. pairs.A: This cat extends learned responses to stimuli that share perceptual features with the original training stimulus. pairs.A: This cat acquires differential responding to stimuli that predict different outcomes through repeated exposure to relevant contingencies. pairs.A: This cat exhibits a fixed action pattern when small rapidly moving objects cross its visual field during outdoor activity. pairs.A: This cat circles repeatedly before lying down in a behavior that persists across domesticated environments lacking natural ground substrates. The circling pattern of this cat varies in duration with ambient temperature and surface texture of the resting area. When this cat selects a resting location, the site preference of this cat favors enclosed spaces with elevated vantage over open exposed areas.

pairs.A: This cat produces a reflexive flinch and orientation response to sudden unexpected stimuli regardless of prior habituation to the environment. The startle magnitude of this cat scales with stimulus intensity and inversely with temporal predictability of the event. When this cat experiences repeated identical startling events, the habituation curve of this cat follows a negative exponential until the response stabilizes.

When your cat experiences repeated identical startling events, the habituation curve of your cat follows a negative exponential until the response stabilizes.

pairs.A: This cat excavates substrate using rapid alternating forepaw movements that persist even when no buried object is present underground. pairs.A: This cat carries retrieved objects to a handler using a grip calibrated to avoid damaging the item during transport. pairs.A: This cat uses directed eye contact to solicit attention from human partners in a behavior not commonly observed in wild wolves.
